div.p: The independence assumption is the optimizer's biggest source of errors. It's a pragmatic tradeoff: collecting correlation statistics for every column pair would make ANALYZE too expensive. Production databases collect limited multi-column stats and accept estimation errors on correlated predicates.
P(A AND B) = P(A) × P(B) Assumes events A and B are statistically independent

li.strong: P(A OR B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A)×P(B)

g.text: Circle A = Rows matching predicate A
Circle B = Rows matching predicate B

div.p: Query: WHERE age > 30 AND income > 100K Independence assumes: Age and income unrelated Reality: Income correlates with age!

ul.li: Older people tend to earn more

ol.li: Wrong operator choice → HashJoin spills to disk
Wrong memory allocation → Too small buffer

div.p: PostgreSQL: CREATE STATISTICS s (ndistinct) ON age, income FROM users; Captures correlation between columns
Track actual vs estimated cardinality Adjust statistics or use hints for repeat queries
Use sqrt(P(A) × P(B)) for AND instead of P(A) × P(B) Assumes some positive correlation (safer default)
